title: Adai
description: Enjoy the wholesome goodness of 'Adai', a traditional South Indian crepe made with a mix of lentils and rice. This recipe is crispy on the outside, soft on the inside, and packed with protein, making it perfect for enjoying anytime.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
main_ingredients: Split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal)

ingredients.raw: ½ cup split pigeon peas (toovar dal), soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: ½ cup split pigeon peas , soaked for 1 hour and drained

ingredients.raw: ¼ cup split red lentils (masoor dal), soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: ¼ cup split red lentils , soaked for 1 hour and drained

ingredients.raw: ½ cup split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal), soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: ½ cup split skinless green gram , soaked for 1 hour and drained

ingredients.raw: ¼ cup split Bengal gram (chana dal), soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: ¼ cup split Bengal gram , soaked for 1 hour and drained

ingredients.raw: ¼ cup short grain rice, soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: ¼ cup short grain rice, soaked for 1 hour and drained

steps: Put split pigeon peas into a grinder jar, add split red lentils, split green gram, split Bengal gram, rice, ginger, curry leaves, dried red chillies, cumin seeds, asafoetida, salt and 1½ cups water and grind to fine batter. Transfer into a deep pan and whisk well.

Heat a non-stick tawa. Spread a ladleful of batter into a round not-too-thin disc and drizzle some oil around. Cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes. Flip and cook for 2-3 minutes more. Make more adais similarly with the remaining batter.
Transfer the adais onto individual serving plates and serve hot with coconut chutney.
tags: Split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal)
